title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Molly and Jules Davis decide to convert their inherited property, Monkswell Manor, into a guesthouse to establish a permanent home. Molly plans to manage the business by raising livestock and growing vegetables, with the intention of eventually hiring a maid. To attract tenants, the couple places advertisements in *The Times* and local newspapers.

On the day the first guests are scheduled to arrive, Jules leaves early to search for barbed wire while Molly walks to a village two miles away to purchase supplies. As a blizzard approaches, Molly experiences intense loneliness and anxiety while waiting in the empty house. She inspects the rooms prepared for their guests: a south-facing room with redwood furniture for Mrs. Boyle, a blue room with oak furniture for Colonel Metcalf, and an east-side room with a bay window for Mr. Lane.

Jules returns late after failing to find the necessary wire mesh. The couple discusses their upcoming guests; while Colonel Metcalf has sent word that he will arrive the following day, they expect Mr. Lane, whom Jules suspects is an artist, to arrive for dinner. Molly expresses concern about their lack of experience as hosts, but she plans to mitigate the risk of non-payment by holding guests' luggage. While preparing a meal of Welsh cheese, Molly describes the difficulties of managing a breakfast service. The couple then listens to a radio report regarding a stalemate in foreign affairs and a murder on Paddington Carlisle Street. Disturbed by the news and the weather, Molly suggests delaying the start of the bed and breakfast until spring.
